nameMeanings.name: Dickinson
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: variant of Dickenson , a patronymic from the Middle English personal name Dicun a pet form of Richard (see Dicken ) + son.
History: Jonathan Dickinson, the first president of the College of New Jersey (now Princeton), was born in Hatfield, MA, in 1688. Both his parents came from pioneer Connecticut Valley stock.

variants.name: Dickenson
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Dicun, a pet form of Richard (see Dicken ) + son.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Dickinson was born in 1452, in Cumberland, England, her father, Hugh Dickinson, was 30 and her mother, Agnes De Swillington, was 22. She married James Pye Pay in 1470, in Derbyshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 1 son. She died on 30 September 1506, in England, United Kingdom, at the age of 54.

fr.High Bentham, North Yorkshire, England: High Bentham, North Yorkshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni
fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Dickinson est née en 1452, à Cumberland, Angleterre, son père, Hugh Dickinson, avait 30 ans et sa mère, Agnes De Swillington, avait 22 ans. Elle a épousé James Pye Pay en 1470, à Derbyshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils. Elle est décédée le 30 septembre 1506, à Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 54 ans.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени James Pye Pay в 1470 г. в местности дирбишир, Англия, Великобритания. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын.
